lint.details: 631 total errors across entire codebase, but all are pre-existing (E402 in scripts due to sys.path pattern, F541 f-strings without placeholders, F401 unused imports, E712 boolean comparisons). 15 errors in milestone-touched files, all non-blocking and consistent with existing codebase patterns. No new lint regressions introduced.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Stale backup file src/services/kline_updater.py.backup contains old retention values (365 days). Could confuse future developers.
DB trade_calendar not actually backfilled to 2021 due to pre-existing TuShare IP rate limit error. Code is correct and ready — will populate when API access is restored.
extend_trade_calendar.py will crash with TypeError if trade_calendar table is completely empty (None[0] access). Edge case only on fresh empty DB.
update_trade_calendar() re-fetches full 2021+ range every scheduled run. Idempotent but slightly inefficient.
Hardcoded date range start_date='20260101', end_date='20260325' in fetch_index_constituents() will become stale if script is re-run later.
Direct access to tushare_client.pro.index_weight() bypasses TushareClient's _request_with_retry wrapper and rate limiter.
Commit 06d40e6 bundles 13 unrelated file changes alongside the feature's 2 files. Non-atomic commit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify TuShare API access convention: 'Always use TushareClient wrapper methods. Never call .pro.* directly — this bypasses rate limiting and retry logic.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: infra-expand-stock-universe worker accessed tushare_client.pro.index_weight() directly, bypassing _request_with_retry(). While not practically impactful (only 3 calls), it sets a precedent that could cause rate limit issues in higher-volume scripts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for script date parameters: 'Scripts that fetch historical data should compute date ranges dynamically (e.g., end_date=today) rather than hardcoding specific dates that become stale.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: infra-expand-stock-universe hardcoded '20260101'-'20260325', and infra-trade-calendar-extension has a hardcoded log message with '2026'. Both will become misleading/stale over time.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml doesn't cover the ashare subproject (only quant-dashboard)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — the ashare project has its own .factory/services.yaml at ashare/.factory/services.yaml which is correctly configured. The root services.yaml is for quant-dashboard.

rejectedObservations.observation: backend-worker skill is oriented toward quant-dashboard rather than ashare
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — the skill is general-purpose and workers successfully adapted. Changing the skill would be too broad a change based on one milestone's evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md doesn't mention retention values (1825 days daily, 365 days 30-min)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — retention values are implementation details in code with clear docstrings. AGENTS.md is for conventions and boundaries, not implementation specifics.

rejectedObservations.observation: environment.md and architecture.md reference stale stock/index counts
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — checked the actual files; they don't contain specific stock counts ('422' or '5 indices'). The library files are already generic.
